title: Revisiting Deepfake Detection: Chronological Continual Learning and the Limits of Generalization

methodology.data_extraction: Custom batch extraction simulating real-world data stream scenarios
methodology.model_approach: Lightweight models for real-time performance on smartphones
methodology.training_data: Chronological simulation using datasets ordered by time

results.key_finding: While some methods retained knowledge of past generators, none exhibited strong generalization to future ones

criticisms_of_prior_work: Utilize a very limited evaluation set (≤2K samples)
Employ a not chronological sequence of deepfake generators
Do not address DFD as a real-world scenario

proposed_solution.approach: Pipeline that chronologically simulates deepfake scenarios over extended periods
proposed_solution.strategy: Simulate sequence of tasks as random sample from datastream
goals: Avoid choice bias on generator sequence
Maintain consistent detector size, speed, and memory usage
Focus on data, training, and inference efficiency
